2009-02-01.title: Guidelines on Bailouts Said to Be in the Works in Europe
2009-02-01.abstract: The European Central Bank is working on guidelines to prevent bailout plans from one country from being significantly more generous than plans from another.

2009-02-01.title: An Island Divided
2009-02-01.abstract: After days of deadly antigovernment protests, two politicians now claim to run Madagascar.

2009-02-01.title: 15 Die in Blaze at a Restaurant in Eastern China
2009-02-01.abstract: A fireworks blast set off a blaze in a restaurant and bar in eastern China on Saturday night, killing at least 15 people and injuring at least 22 others, according to state-run news outlets.

2009-02-01.title: Election Reaction: U.S. Embassy and U.S. Military
2009-02-01.abstract: A press release from the U.S. embassy and the American military in Iraq.

2009-02-01.title: Election Reaction: Iraqi Journalists’ Experiences
2009-02-01.abstract: We asked Iraqi journalists working for The New York Times in Iraq to tell us about their experience of polling day yesterday, as voters.

2009-02-02.title: Idling Factories Keep the Kremlin Occupied
2009-02-02.abstract: Tens of thousands of workers in the heart of Russia’s automotive region are out of work after several large factories there temporarily closed.

2009-02-02.title: More Than 6 Killed by Shelling at a Sri Lanka Hospital
2009-02-02.abstract: The attack came as the Sri Lankan military pushed further into an area controlled by the separatist Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam.

2009-02-02.title: Iceland Names New Prime Minister
2009-02-02.abstract: Johanna Sigurdardottir is a political veteran who has served for long periods as social affairs minister. She also appears to be the modern world’s first openly gay head of government.

2009-02-02.title: Russian Leaders Attend Installation of Orthodox Patriarch
2009-02-02.abstract: Patriarch Kirill I was elected last week to succeed Aleksy II, who had led the Russian Orthodox Church out of the Soviet era and died in Moscow on Dec. 5.

2009-02-02.title: Battle Royal Over Chief for a Czech State of Mind
2009-02-02.abstract: The whimsical, and fictional, kingdom of Wallachia, in the northeast corner of the Czech Republic, has become entangled in a legal battle over who controls it.

2009-02-03.title: Shoe Is Thrown at Chinese Premier
2009-02-03.abstract: A protester threw an athletic shoe at the Chinese prime minister, Wen Jiabao, during his speech at Cambridge University’s concert hall on Monday.

2009-02-03.title: Bolivia’s Lithium Reserves
2009-02-03.abstract: Almost half of the world’s lithium, the mineral used in electric-car batteries, is found in Bolivia — a country that may not be willing to surrender it easily.

2009-02-03.title: Australian Court Jails Muslim Cleric
2009-02-03.abstract: A Muslim cleric convicted of forming a terrorist cell in Australia was sentenced Tuesday to 15 years in jail, bringing the country’s largest terrorism trial to a close.

2009-02-03.title: North Korean Activity Stirs Fears of a Missile Test
2009-02-03.abstract: North Korea has been moving what appear to be components of a long-range missile to a newly constructed launch site.

2009-02-03.title: Somalia: African Union Peacekeepers Are Accused of Firing on Civilians
2009-02-03.abstract: At least 20 people were killed and dozens wounded after a roadside bomb exploded on Monday in Mogadishu, the capital, and African Union forces fired in response, Somali officials said. The blast hit a truck for African Union peacekeepers near one of the union’s bases. Abdifitah Ibrahim Shaweye, deputy mayor of Mogadishu, said the peacekeepers then fired into a crowd of civilians, killing 39. A spokesman for the peacekeepers denied that account, saying the soldiers were responding to small-arms fire after the blast.

2009-02-04.title: Saudis Issue List of 85 Terrorism Suspects
2009-02-04.abstract: Eleven Saudis who were released from Guantánamo Bay and then passed through a Saudi rehabilitation program are now believed to have joined terrorist groups.

2009-02-04.title: As Iraqis Tally Votes, Former Leader Re-emerges
2009-02-04.abstract: Ayad Allawi, the first prime minister selected after the Americans returned power to Iraqis in 2004, made a comeback in the provincial elections.

2009-02-04.title: Sudan: U.S. Envoy to U.N. Speaks Out on Darfur
2009-02-04.abstract: Susan E. Rice, the American ambassador to the United Nations, called on Sudan to protect civilians in Muhajiriya, in Darfur, where fighting has erupted.

2009-02-04.title: China: Shoe-Hurling at Premier Is Shown on TV
2009-02-04.abstract: Chinese state television showed scenes of a protester tossing a shoe at its prime minister, a break from its practice of ignoring potential embarrassments to its leaders.

2009-02-04.title: On Iran, Obama Plans Talk and Some Toughness
2009-02-04.abstract: While President Obama is expected to soften the line against talking to Iran, he may try to toughen sanctions.

2009-02-05.title: Gaza War Created Rift Between Israel and Turkey
2009-02-05.abstract: Turkey, Israel’s closest Muslim ally, protested the war with a month of angry remarks capped when its prime minister stalked off the stage during a debate with the Israeli president.

2009-02-05.title: Citing U.S., British Court Blocks Data on Suspect
2009-02-05.abstract: The court said the U.S. threatened to stop sharing intelligence with Britain if it released information on a man who says he was tortured in American custody.

2009-02-05.title: Iraqi Government Aims to Calm Tensions in Anbar Over Allegations of Election Fraud
2009-02-05.abstract: A tribal leader in Anbar Province has threatened to take up arms against a rival party whose officials he accused of manipulating vote tallies.

2009-02-05.title: Fossils of Largest Snake Give Hint of Hot Earth
2009-02-05.abstract: The discovery of giant snake fossils in northeast Colombia may help clarify how hot the tropics became during an era when the planet was much warmer than it is now.

2009-02-05.title: Chinese Learn Limits of Online Freedom as the Filter Tightens
2009-02-05.abstract: The recent censoring of a tongue-in-cheek variety show has provoked howls from China’s so-called netizens, critical of their government’s control of the Web.

2009-02-06.title: A New Tongue to Win Hearts and Minds
2009-02-06.abstract: Nearly 200 Egyptian teenagers from poor families have learned English from a U.S. State Department program designed to promote understanding from the bottom up.

2009-02-06.title: Japan’s Big-Works Stimulus Is Lesson
2009-02-06.abstract: Over two decades, Japan accumulated enormous debt while failing to generate a convincing recovery.

2009-02-06.title: Fertility Drugs and Ovarian Cancer Not Linked, Study Says
2009-02-06.abstract: A Danish study found “no convincing association” with the cancer, though researchers said they would continue to follow their subjects to see if risk increased with age.

2009-02-06.title: Germans Corroborate Reports of Nazi Doctor’s Death in Egypt
2009-02-06.abstract: German investigators said that they had information corroborating reports that Aribert Heim, the concentration camp doctor, had died in Egypt in 1992.

2009-02-06.title: Possible Link Between Dam and China Quake
2009-02-06.abstract: The weight of a reservoir a mile from a major fault may have triggered the 2008 earthquake in Sichuan Province.

2009-02-07.title: Italy Moves to Keep Alive a Woman in a Coma
2009-02-07.abstract: The move circumvents a high court decision and ignores a warning from the Italian president that he would refuse to sign the decree.

2009-02-07.title: U.S. Military Violated Security Agreement Twice in 2 Weeks, Iraqi Leaders Say
2009-02-07.abstract: Iraqi leaders charge that the American military attacked Iraqi criminal suspects without coordinating with Iraqi security forces.

2009-02-07.title: Gaza: U.N. Agency Stops Its Imports
2009-02-07.abstract: The United Nations agency said it was suspending imports of goods because Hamas had twice stolen aid from it.

2009-02-07.title: Israel: Gaza-Bound Activists Deported
2009-02-07.abstract: Israeli officials said they had deported most of the activists who were on a Gaza-bound ship carrying humanitarian aid that was intercepted a day earlier by the Israeli Navy.

2009-02-07.title: Ecuador: Port Deal Falls Through
2009-02-07.abstract: Hutchison Whampoa, a conglomerate based in Hong Kong, pulled out of a deal to operate a cargo port in the city of Manta.

2009-02-08.title: Corrections
2009-02-08.abstract: An article on Jan. 25 about Pope Benedict XVI’s revoking the excommunication of four schismatic bishops referred incorrectly to the use of a Good Friday prayer that calls for the conversion of Jews. The prayer is part of a service — not a Mass — on Good Friday, according to a traditional version of the Latin Mass, known as the Tridentine rite. (No Mass is said on Good Friday.).

2009-02-08.title: Death Toll in Australian Fires Climbs to 131
2009-02-08.abstract: The police say that at least 750 homes have been destroyed in the country’s deadliest wildfires in decades.

2009-02-08.title: Trial Date Set for Iraqi ‘Shoe-Thrower’
2009-02-08.abstract: The Iraqi journalist who threw his shoes at the former American president, George Bush, will go on trial on Feb. 19, charged with assaulting a foreign leader.

2009-02-08.title: Holbrooke Says Afghan War ‘Tougher Than Iraq’
2009-02-08.abstract: “There is no magic formula in Afghanistan,” Richard C. Holbrooke told an audience of European policy makers.

2009-02-08.title: Two Taliban Are Killed in Revenge by Afghans
2009-02-08.abstract: In an unusual response to a local politician’s assassination, villagers captured and killed two members of the Taliban.

2009-02-09.title: Radical Books Raise Fears in Indonesia of Spread of Militants’ Ideas
2009-02-09.abstract: Counterterrorism experts fear a small but growing group of publishers are proof of how interconnected the Jemaah Islamiyah movement is in Indonesia.

2009-02-09.title: Venezuelans Campaign on Term Limits Amid Rising Tension
2009-02-09.abstract: In the days before a referendum that will determine whether President Hugo Chávez can run for re-election indefinitely, campaigning has taken on a noticeable edge.

2009-02-09.title: Attack on Women at an Indian Bar Intensifies a Clash of Cultures
2009-02-09.abstract: A mob attack on women drinking in a college-town bar is laying bare the limits of freedom for young Indian women.

2009-02-09.title: A Secularist Finds Sway in a Hub of Shiite Islam
2009-02-09.abstract: Yousef Majid al-Habboubi soundly defeated candidates from the religious parties that controlled Karbala Province in Iraq, gaining nearly twice as many votes as his two closest competitors.

2009-02-09.title: Claim in Africa About a Leader Is Recanted
2009-02-09.abstract: A newspaper said a 24-year-old woman who claimed to be pregnant by President Kgalema Motlanthe had lied about her story.

2009-02-10.title: Israeli Vote Brings Push for Release of a Soldier
2009-02-10.abstract: The return of the soldier, who was seized by Hamas in June 2006, would close a circle for the exiting Prime Minister Ehud Olmert, who came to power that May.

2009-02-10.title: Taliban Haven in Pakistani City Raises Fears
2009-02-10.abstract: Violence in southern Afghanistan suggests a flow of arms, fighters and money from the Pakistani city of Quetta.

2009-02-10.title: After Iraqi Elections, Next Big Test Is Acceptance
2009-02-10.abstract: Iraq’s elections passed with strikingly little mayhem, but the results have riled those vying for power.

2009-02-10.title: For Russia’s Migrants, Economic Despair Douses Flickers of Hope
2009-02-10.abstract: Russia’s millions of migrants are facing increasing hardship as the country enters its worst economic decline since the 1998 ruble collapse.

2009-02-10.title: Video of Beheading of Pole Held by Taliban Is Real, Official Says
2009-02-10.abstract: The Polish foreign minister demanded that the killers of Polish geologist abducted by the Taliban in Pakistan be brought to justice.

2009-02-11.title: The Dissonant Undertones of M.I.A.
2009-02-11.abstract: Critics say the rapper M.I.A. is an apologist for the Tamil Tiger rebels fighting in Sri Lanka’s civil war.

2009-02-11.title: U.S. Prepares to Broach Hard Issues With China
2009-02-11.abstract: The Obama administration plans to realign the United States’ relationship with China by putting more emphasis on climate change, energy and human rights.

2009-02-11.title: Correction
2009-02-11.abstract: An article on Friday about scientific studies that suggested the weight of a large manmade reservoir might have triggered the devastating May 12 earthquake in China’s Sichuan province misspelled the name of the quake’s geological fault line. It is Wenchuan, not Wenchaun.

2009-02-11.title: U.N. Chief Says Israel Is Blocking Most Gaza Aid
2009-02-11.abstract: Ban Ki-moon repeated Tuesday his demand that Israel allow significantly more humanitarian aid into the beleaguered Gaza Strip.

2009-02-11.title: In Peacekeeping, a Muddling of the Mission
2009-02-11.abstract: After failing to prevent massacres in Rwanda and Srebrenica, Bosnia, experts are beginning to question whether the U.N.’s mission is heading toward crisis.

2009-02-12.title: Laid-Off Foreigners Flee as Dubai Spirals Down
2009-02-12.abstract: As layoffs hit Dubai’s foreign workers, their departure is making parts of the city look like a ghost town.

2009-02-12.title: In Pakistan, U.S. Special Envoy Finds Discontent
2009-02-12.abstract: Richard C. Holbrooke heard a familiar list of requests for more money and arms from Pakistan’s leadership, and a litany of complaints about American airstrikes.

2009-02-12.title: Obama Weighs Adding Troops in Afghanistan
2009-02-12.abstract: The president must decide whether to approve more troops for Afghanistan before a new strategy has been set.

2009-02-12.title: Italy: First Report on High-Profile Death
2009-02-12.abstract: A preliminary autopsy on the woman at the center of a right-to-die case in Italy has found that the woman, died Monday of cardiac arrest because of dehydration.

2009-02-12.title: Mexico: 7 Arrested in Cancún Killing
2009-02-12.abstract: The authorities said Wednesday that they had arrested seven suspects in the Feb. 3 murder of the retired general Mauro Enrique Tello.

2009-02-13.title: Israel Opens the Gaza Border for 25,000 Carnations, Bound for Europe
2009-02-13.abstract: Just in time for Valentine’s Day, Israel allowed the export of a shipment of flowers from Hamas-run Gaza on Thursday for the first time in roughly a year.

2009-02-13.title: Violence Across Iraq Kills 13, Including a Sunni Politician
2009-02-13.abstract: 39 Iraqis were also wounded in a spate of attacks that included the assassination of a Sunni Arab political leader in the violent northern city of Mosul.

2009-02-13.title: Brazilian Police Arrest Dozens in Drug Rings
2009-02-13.abstract: The Brazilian police said Thursday that it arrested 55 people involved in two crime rings that were smuggling drugs into the country from Europe.

2009-02-13.title: Mayor Aims to Add Spark to Flagging Sex Lives
2009-02-13.abstract: With elections looming, Mexico City’s mayor, Marcelo Ebrard, is giving Viagra away to poor, older men.

2009-02-13.title: Taiwan’s Low Profile May Aid Its Goals
2009-02-13.abstract: Taiwan’s president said he was not concerned that Taiwan was low on Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton’s list of priorities.

2009-02-14.title: Britain: After Remark, Prince Must Attend Training
2009-02-14.abstract: Prince Harry has been ordered to attend a British Army diversity training course after a video surfaced last month in which he referred to a fellow military cadet as a “Paki.”

2009-02-14.title: Italy: American Student Testifies in Murder Trial
2009-02-14.abstract: An American college student on trial in the murder of her housemate took the stand for the first time on Friday, Italian news media reported.

2009-02-14.title: United Nations: Extension for Abkhazia Border Unit
2009-02-14.abstract: The United Nations Security Council voted 15-0 on Friday to keep cease-fire observers along the border of Georgia’s breakaway region of Abkhazia for another four months.

2009-02-14.title: British Officers in ’05 Killing in Subway Won’t Be Charged
2009-02-14.abstract: The decision comes after a new review of a police shooting that killed a Brazilian man mistakenly identified as a terrorist bomber.

2009-02-14.title: Impairing the European Union, Gibe by Gibe
2009-02-14.abstract: The bitterness at the top of the union is damaging the alliance’s ability to cope with the economic crisis.

2009-02-15.title: Chávez Reaches Out to Obama Ahead of Vote
2009-02-15.abstract: President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela said that he was ready to engage in direct talks with President Obama in a bid to repair relations with the U.S.

2009-02-15.title: Correction
2009-02-15.abstract: An article last Sunday about the problems confronting Hamid Karzai, the president of Afghanistan, misidentified the type of hat he is often seen wearing. It is a karakul, not a fez.

2009-02-15.title: Aide’s Arrest in Zimbabwe Draws Fire of Premier
2009-02-15.abstract: Prime Minister Morgan Tsvangirai called the arrest of a close political ally an attempt to “undermine the spirit and credibility of the unity government.”

2009-02-15.title: Venezuelans Voting on Lifting Term Limits
2009-02-15.abstract: A referendum could open the way for President Hugo Chávez to hold onto power well into the next decade.

2009-02-15.title: Venezuela Votes on Referendum
2009-02-15.abstract: Venezuelans went to the polls to vote on a referendum that would open the door for President Hugo Chávez to seek power indefinitely.

2009-02-16.title: U.S. Envoy Reaches Out to Iran in Afghan Visit
2009-02-16.abstract: Richard Holbrooke, President Obama’s top envoy to Afghanistan, declared that Iran should play a vital role helping stabilize the war-torn country.

2009-02-16.title: Pakistan and Taliban Appear Near Deal
2009-02-16.abstract: In a violent region of northern Pakistan, militants declared a 10-day cease-fire and the government said it was willing to accept the imposition of Islamic law.

2009-02-16.title: British Detainee to Return Home
2009-02-16.abstract: A British terrorism suspect, Binyam Mohamed, who says he was tortured while in American custody, is preparing to return home this week.

2009-02-16.title: Mexico Gunmen Kill Officer and 11 Others
2009-02-16.abstract: Gunmen killed a policeman in an elite squad formed in the state of Tabasco to address growing violence, much of which is related to drug-trafficking cartels.

2009-02-16.title: In Quest for Equal Rights, Muslim Women’s Meeting Turns to Islam’s Tenets
2009-02-16.abstract: Frustrations with religious edicts, such as bans on yoga and styles of dress, drew several hundred Muslim women to a conference in Malaysia over the weekend.

2009-02-17.title: A Big Wall Falls in China to Make Room for Mickey
2009-02-17.abstract: Trading snow dragons for Snow White, a Disney licensing company took over the Harbin Ice Lantern Festival, China’s most popular wintertime attraction.

2009-02-17.title: Stephen Kim Sou-hwan, Cardinal, Dies at 86
2009-02-17.abstract: Cardinal Kim was South Korea’s first cardinal and a tireless advocate for democracy who stood up to a series of military dictators.

2009-02-17.title: Chávez Looks Beyond 2013 as He Faces Serious Challenges
2009-02-17.abstract: Major obstacles to Venezuelan President Hugo Chávez’s revolutionary dream lie ahead, in the form of sagging oil prices and a sizable opposition.

2009-02-17.title: Amid Scandals, Questions of Where the Pope’s Focus Lies
2009-02-17.abstract: Successive scandals have stirred a debate on whether Pope Benedict XVI’s focus on doctrine and perceived insensitivity to tone are alienating mainstream Catholics.

2009-02-17.title: Russia: 4 Governors Are Replaced
2009-02-17.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev installed new leadership in regions that might be susceptible to social unrest because of the financial crisis.

2009-02-18.title: Putting Stamp on Afghan War, Obama Will Send 17,000 Troops
2009-02-18.abstract: The order will add nearly 50 percent to the 36,000 American troops already there.

2009-02-18.title: Corrections
2009-02-18.abstract: An article on Feb. 7 about an Italian man who was detained by bewildered Iraqi police after they found him wandering around Falluja as a tourist, without guards or a translator, rendered part of an Italian phrase incorrectly. It is bel viaggio (nice trip), not bella viaggio.

2009-02-18.title: Iraq Says It Is Interrogating 4 Men Returned by U.S. From Guantánamo Prison
2009-02-18.abstract: The prisoners, who had been arrested in Afghanistan, were sent to Iraq more than a month ago, according to a senior American defense official.

2009-02-18.title: Egypt’s Critics Have a Voice, but Never the Last Word
2009-02-18.abstract: In Egypt, there is relative freedom to criticize the government, but not to act against it.

2009-02-18.title: Russia: Senior Judge Quits After Son Is Charged
2009-02-18.abstract: A senior Supreme Court judge whose son has been charged with accepting a bribe of almost $30,000 resigned, a court spokesman said.

2009-02-19.title: Britain: Jury Dismissed in Airliner Trial
2009-02-19.abstract: The judge in a trial of eight men accused of plotting to blow up several trans-Atlantic airliners discharged the jury after only one full day of testimony.

2009-02-19.title: Armed With Little but Resolve, and Defending a Hollowed Village
2009-02-19.abstract: Militias have sprung up in isolated villages in Congo in an attempt to defend against the Lord’s Resistance Army rebels.

2009-02-19.title: New Lessons for the Army on Iraq Duty
2009-02-19.abstract: An after-action review of the military’s last 15-month rotation in northern Iraq offers lessons to the Obama administration as it prepares for further reductions of American troops.

2009-02-19.title: Egyptian Political Dissident, Imprisoned for Years, Is Suddenly Released
2009-02-19.abstract: The U.S. and Europe had long pressed for the release of Ayman Nour, a one-time presidential candidate.

2009-02-19.title: Drug Tie Seen to Protests in Mexico
2009-02-19.abstract: Authorities say they see the hidden hand of drug traffickers in a recent wave of demonstrations protesting the presence of army troops across northern Mexico.

2009-02-20.title: Congo: Military to Press On With Hunt for Hutu Rebels
2009-02-20.abstract: The Congolese military said Thursday that its operations against Hutu rebels in eastern Congo would continue after Rwanda pulled its forces out at the end of the month.

2009-02-20.title: Somalia: A Disagreement Over Peacekeepers
2009-02-20.abstract: More than 300 clerics and clan elders gathering in the capital, Mogadishu, called Thursday for African Union peacekeepers to leave Somalia within 120 days.

2009-02-20.title: China: Beijing Warns Tibet’s Clergy to Avoid Politics
2009-02-20.abstract: A senior Communist Party official has warned monks and nuns to refrain from engaging in political activity before the first anniversary of the anti-Chinese uprising last March.

2009-02-20.title: Czech Republic: Scorn for the European Union
2009-02-20.abstract: President Vaclav Klaus on Thursday indirectly compared the union to the former Soviet Union, saying that there was a similar uniformity of thought.

2009-02-20.title: France: Wage Offer Aimed at Calming Island’s Protests
2009-02-20.abstract: After weeks of strikes over the cost of living on the French Caribbean island of Guadeloupe, Prime Minister François Fillon proposed increasing income for the poor by about $250 a month.

2009-02-21.title: Netanyahu, Once Hawkish, Now Touts Pragmatism
2009-02-21.abstract: Benjamin Netanyahu, picked to lead Israel, called on the centrists and the left to form a unity government.

2009-02-21.title: In a Letter, a Leader of Hamas Makes an Appeal to Obama
2009-02-21.abstract: Ahmed Yousef, the Hamas deputy foreign minister, has asked President Obama to engage with his militant movement, rather than marginalize it.

2009-02-21.title: Telling Twins Apart Takes New Meaning in Berlin Heist
2009-02-21.abstract: Investigators at the crime scene of a multimillion-dollar jewelry heist found DNA leading to not one, but two suspects: a pair of twins.

2009-02-21.title: With New Name and Mission, the Infamous Abu Ghraib Prison Is to Reopen
2009-02-21.abstract: The Iraqi government says it sorely needs Abu Ghraib — now Baghdad Central Prison — because of overcrowding at prisons and continued threats to security.

2009-02-21.title: NATO Allies Offer Support for Afghans
2009-02-21.abstract: Few allies were willing to offer significant numbers of additional combat troops for Afghanistan, but some might deploy more civilians to train local security forces and build the country’s economy.

2009-02-22.title: Georgia Offers Sideburns and a Disco Beat as Payback for a War
2009-02-22.abstract: Georgia has taken the fight with its northern neighbor to the disco floor, with plans to present a tune that jabs at Vladimir V. Putin at Europe’s premier song contest.

2009-02-22.title: Turkish Premier Reaches Out in an Important Kurdish City
2009-02-22.abstract: The Turkish prime minister, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, took his message of inclusion to the Kurdish heartland on Saturday, calling its people “first-class citizens.”

2009-02-22.title: Taliban Truce Seems in Flux in Pakistan
2009-02-22.abstract: A top Taliban leader indicated the truce entered with Islamabad in exchange for Islamic law may be temporary.

2009-02-22.title: Rocket Fired From Lebanon Strikes Northern Israel
2009-02-22.abstract: A Katyusha rocket fired from southern Lebanon hit a village in northern Israel early Saturday, wounding a woman.

2009-02-23.title: Bomb Blast Kills Tourist in Cairo
2009-02-23.abstract: The attack on Hussein Square, a main tourist stop in Cairo, killed a French woman.

2009-02-23.title: Mystery of the ‘Land of Twins’: Something in the Water? Mengele?
2009-02-23.abstract: Geneticists have no explanation for the 38 pairs of twins living in a one-and-a-half square mile area in Brazil, the “twins capital of the world.”

2009-02-23.title: Duo’s Second Prison Escape Embarrasses Greek Officials
2009-02-23.abstract: The escape by Vassilis Paleokostas and Alket Rizai came one day before both were to face trial for the first escape from Korydallos prison in 2006.

2009-02-23.title: U.S. Unit Secretly in Pakistan Lends Ally Support
2009-02-23.abstract: American military advisers are working in Pakistan to help its armed forces battle Al Qaeda and the Taliban in lawless tribal areas.

2009-02-23.title: China Fears Tremors as Jobs Vanish From Coast
2009-02-23.abstract: Jobs on the once-booming Chinese coast are vanishing at a stunning pace, leaving millions without work.

2009-02-24.title: U.S. to Give $900 Million in Gaza Aid, Officials Say
2009-02-24.abstract: The aid would be funneled through nongovernmental organizations rather than through Hamas.

2009-02-24.title: From a Carrier, Another View of America’s Air War in Afghanistan
2009-02-24.abstract: The U.S. military is experiencing the limitations of its increasing reliance on airstrikes, which have kindled growing fury over civilian casualties.

2009-02-24.title: A Russian City’s Wounds Are Dressed in Opera Garb
2009-02-24.abstract: In a tiny workshop in Russia’s southernmost stronghold, the seamstresses of Vladikavkaz live the rhythm of a distant curtain.

2009-02-24.title: Saint Laurent Art Sale Brings In $264 Million
2009-02-24.abstract: The first round of auctions of items from Yves Saint Laurent’s art collection brought record prices for works by several artists, including Henri Matisse.

2009-02-24.title: The Iraq Museum Reopens
2009-02-24.abstract: The museum whose looting in 2003 became a symbol of the chaos that followed the American invasion officially reopened on Monday.

2009-02-25.title: Obama Favoring Mid-2010 Pullout in Iraq, Aides Say
2009-02-25.abstract: The president was prepared to extend the timetable by three months to respond to ground commanders’ concerns, officials said.

2009-02-25.title: Strikes Worsen Qaeda Threat, Pakistan Says
2009-02-25.abstract: Military raids have reduced Al Qaeda’s reach but heightened its threat to Pakistan, officials said.

2009-02-25.title: China’s Answer to a Crime Includes Amateur Sleuths
2009-02-25.abstract: Responding to an outcry on the Internet, the Chinese government appointed a citizen committee to investigate a death in prison.

2009-02-25.title: Aboard the U.S.S. Theodore Roosevelt
2009-02-25.abstract: An aircraft carrier provides air support for American and NATO ground troops fighting in Afghanistan.

2009-02-25.title: Syria Discloses Missile Facility, Europeans Say
2009-02-25.abstract: The facility is on the ruins of a possible nuclear reactor that Israeli warplanes bombed in 2007, European diplomats quoted a Syrian official as saying.

2009-02-26.title: Falling Revenues Threaten Rebuilding in Iraq
2009-02-26.abstract: The financial crisis and falling oil prices will make it harder for Iraq to pay for services and could affect U.S. plans for withdrawal.

2009-02-26.title: Son’s Death Recasts Image of a British Leader
2009-02-26.abstract: The life and death of David Cameron’s young disabled son have helped bridge a social gap between him and Britain.

2009-02-26.title: China Fails to Halt the Sale of Looted Relics at a Paris Auction
2009-02-26.abstract: Anonymous bidders bought two bronze animal heads from the 18th century that had been in the collection of the French fashion designer Yves Saint Laurent.

2009-02-26.title: C.I.A. Pakistan Campaign Is Working, Director Says
2009-02-26.abstract: Leon E. Panetta, the new C.I.A. director, cited successes in killing a cadre of Al Qaeda’s leadership.

2009-02-26.title: Lawmaker Leaving Iraq Is Detained, Then Freed
2009-02-26.abstract: Mohammad al-Daini has been stripped of his immunity in connection with accusations of killings, kidnappings and a bombing of Parliament.

2009-02-27.title: Book Casts Harsh Light on Ex-Hostage in Colombia
2009-02-27.abstract: Ingrid Betancourt, a Colombian politician, is depicted as selfish and haughty in a memoir by fellow hostages held with her.

2009-02-27.title: Malawi: Former President Is Arrested
2009-02-27.abstract: Former President Bakili Muluzi was arrested and charged with siphoning more than $11 million of international donor money into his personal bank account.

2009-02-27.title: Iranian Rebukes U.S. Counterpart
2009-02-27.abstract: A passing remark from the new American ambassador to the United Nations, Susan Rice, prompted a rebuke from her Iranian counterpart.

2009-02-27.title: The Netherlands: Victims’ Nationalities
2009-02-27.abstract: The nine people who died when a Turkish Airlines jet crash-landed on Wednesday were four Americans and five Turks, Dutch officials said.

2009-02-27.title: Iraq Hands Death Penalty to 28 Cultists for Attacks
2009-02-27.abstract: A messianic Shiite fringe group has taken aim at Shiite pilgrims and fought American and Iraqi security forces in brutal clashes.

2009-02-28.title: U.N. Official Faults Evidence and Foreign States Linked to Guantánamo Interrogations
2009-02-28.abstract: A report says those complicit in the questioning of inmates violated a legal obligation to reject torture and arbitrary detention.

2009-02-28.title: European Leaders to Meet Amid Economic Divisions
2009-02-28.abstract: Tensions between France and the Czech Republic persist, amid doubts about what such a meeting could accomplish.

2009-02-28.title: With Pledges to Troops and Iraqis, Obama Details Pullout
2009-02-28.abstract: President Obama announced that he would withdraw combat forces from Iraq by August 2010 and all remaining American troops by December 2011.

2009-02-28.title: Facing Language Gaps and ‘Flying Trucks,’ U.S. Trains Afghan Pilots
2009-02-28.abstract: The pilots are learning to fly the helicopters of the former Soviet Union as part of a program aimed at giving Afghanistan the ability to defend itself from the skies.

2009-02-28.title: Farmers Find New Reason to Seethe in Argentina
2009-02-28.abstract: Two major newspapers in the country reported that the government was considering nationalizing domestic and foreign grain trading, the country’s most important export industry.
